source_jobs.title: Growth Manager, South Asia
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US



We’re building the world’s leading cross-border fintech for emerging markets - empowering families, fueling local economies, and changing how millions access financial services.



Since our launch in 2019, we have transformed how money moves across borders with our operations spanning 6 out of 7 continents and covering both the busiest and most underserved (remittance) corridors. We tapped into a massive shift - from banks to phones - bringing digital finance to places where traditional systems never reached. The next stage of growth will fuel a network of services and products especially designed to make money more accessible to diaspora communities including local cards, and additional savings and payments features.



We’re growing fast, backed by world-class investors, and scaling a product that blends real world impact with cutting edge fintech innovation.



Here’s a little more:

 - Founded by Harvard grads and 3-time founders who previously built Segovia (acquired) and GiveDirectly (>$300M revenue, fastest-growing NGO in the world)

 - Backed by top investors including Spark Capital, Canaan, Reid Hoffman, Breyer Capital, Unbound, Wamda, and more

 - Built by global top performers from Bain, Google, McKinsey, Uber, Checkout, Bolt, and beyond - we move fast, stay humble, and care deeply about our mission

 - Truly global team - 250+ people, 50+ nationalities, 50+ languages

 - Guided by our values - ownership, impact, humility, and heart - in deed, not just word





THE ROLE



We are looking for a seasoned growth and partnerships professional to lead TTS's expansion across South Asian communities in the US — spanning Indian, Pakistani, Bangladeshi, Nepali, and Sri Lankan diaspora markets.

Reporting to the Head of South Asia, you will own the full growth strategy for the corridor — from building high-value partnership ecosystems to driving customer acquisition and long-term retention. This is a role for someone who brings an existing network, commercial instincts, and the ability to move fast in a high-growth startup environment.




RESPONSIBILITIES



Partnerships & Business Development

 - Identify, negotiate, and close strategic partnerships with community organisations, ethnic media, cultural associations, religious institutions, and remittance-adjacent businesses (travel agents, grocery chains, money exchanges) across key US cities

 - Build and manage a structured partnership pipeline, tracking deal value, activation timelines, and revenue impact

 - Develop co-marketing and referral arrangements that drive measurable customer acquisition at efficient CAC

 - Represent TTS at senior levels with external partners, acting as the face of the corridor



Growth Strategy & Execution

 - Design and execute a multi-channel growth strategy across the South Asia corridor, combining offline community activations, digital campaigns, ambassador programmes and influencer partnerships

 - Source, onboard and manage a network of community champions and brand ambassadors, setting clear KPIs and maintaining ongoing engagement

 - Lead localised campaign planning across communities, ensuring cultural relevance and message resonance

 - Monitor competitive activity and identify whitespace opportunities across communities



Data, Financials & Performance

 - Build a test-and-learn framework across acquisition channels, using data to double down on what works and kill what doesn't

 - Report regularly on growth metrics, partnership performance and market opportunities




REQUIREMENTS

 - 6–8 years of experience in growth, partnerships, business development or community marketing — ideally within fintech or a consumer startup; serving diaspora communities a plus

 - A proven track record of building and closing partnerships that delivered measurable commercial outcomes

 - Deep existing networks within South Asian communities in the US — you are known and trusted in these spaces

ABOUT THE ROLE



Taptap Send is building a new institutional payments business. We move money across 80+ emerging market corridors and are now opening that infrastructure to institutional clients — crypto exchanges, fintechs, MTOs, and corporate treasury teams. This is an early commercial hire into that business.




THE ROLE



You'll own a pipeline of institutional partners from first contact through to live. You'll work closely with trading and ops to structure deals and get clients across the line quickly.




WHAT YOU'LL DO



 - Source and close new institutional partnerships across crypto, fintech, and payments

 - Manage the full BD cycle, outreach, qualification, commercial negotiation, onboarding handoff

 - Represent Taptap Send at industry events and build profile in target segments

 - Work with internal teams to translate client needs into product and corridor priorities

 - Maintain a clean pipeline and report directly to senior leadership




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



 - 3–5 years in BD, partnerships, or sales within payments, crypto, or FX

 - An existing network across crypto exchanges, MTOs, or EM fintechs

 - Comfortable selling a technical product to sophisticated buyers, treasury teams, CFOs, heads of payments

 - Organised, self-directed, and happy working without a large support structure around you




WHAT WE OFFER




 - Competitive base + performance-linked bonus

 - Direct line to senior leadership and real influence over commercial strategy

 - A fast-growing platform with genuine EM depth and strong banking infrastructure behind it

We tapped into a massive shift - from banks to phones - bringing digital finance to places where traditional systems never reached. The next stage of growth will fuel a network of services and products especially designed to make money more accessible to diaspora communities including local cards, and additional savings and payments features.



We’re growing fast, backed by world-class investors, and scaling a product that blends real world impact with cutting edge fintech innovation.



Here’s a little more:

 - Founded by Harvard grads and 3-time founders who previously built Segovia (acquired) and GiveDirectly (>$300M revenue, fastest-growing NGO in the world)

 - Backed by top investors including Spark Capital, Canaan, Reid Hoffman, Breyer Capital, Unbound, Wamda, and more

 - Built by global top performers from Bain, Google, McKinsey, Uber, Checkout, Bolt, and beyond - we move fast, stay humble, and care deeply about our mission

 - Truly global team - 250+ people, 50+ nationalities, 50+ languages

 - Guided by our values - ownership, impact, humility, and heart - in deed, not just word




THE ROLE



Every day, Taptap Send moves money across 80+ market corridors. Behind every transfer is a simple promise: that what we expected to send and receive matches what actually settled at our banking and disbursement partners. Reconciliation is how we keep that promise and how we protect the company's cash and balance sheet as we scale.



We're hiring a Finance Reconciliation Analyst into our Dubai hub to take ownership of this work across 35+ currency corridors. Reporting to the Treasury / Reconciliation lead, you'll own the day-to-day matching of expected versus actual wires, investigate discrepancies, and drive gaps to resolution with partners. You'll work at the intersection of treasury, finance, and operations; and you'll help us move from manual, ad-hoc fixes toward systematic, automated reconciliation.




WHAT YOU'LL DO



 - Own monthly & quarterly reconciliation of expected versus actual funding movements across multiple currency corridors, identifying and documenting genuine discrepancies

 - Investigate missing, partial, or mismatched settlements

 - Manage escalations with banking and disbursement partners, and collaborate closely with respective TaptapSend teams

 - Maintain data integrity across our financial systems and source records

 - Support senior leadership with clear, well-supported analysis on fund reconciliation and balance sheet

 - Drive process improvement and automation; helping build scalable reconciliation infrastructure rather than relying on manual workarounds




WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



 - 1-3 years of experience in one of: external audit / assurance (Big 4 or similar), or payment / finance operations at a fintech, bank, or fast-growing startup

 - Strong comfort with numbers and exceptional attention to detail paired with an hands-on execution based mindset. You like doing the work, not just reviewing it

 - A genuine sense of ownership; you chase loose ends until they're fully resolved

 - Solid stakeholder management: you can hold a partner to account, collaborate cross-functionally, and communicate clearly with senior management

 - Strong Excel / Google Sheets skills; SQL experience (or a real appetite to learn it quickly) is a strong plus

 - Interest in process improvement, automation, and using AI tooling to make finance processes more efficient

 - Based in or willing to relocate to Dubai

You’ll set the technical bar for Analytics Engineering at TTS. That means owning our thorniest modelling problems, ensuring that trusted, self-serve data is default across the company. DIRECTOR OF TRADING


Job Title: Director of Trading
Location: London (hybrid)
Reports to: Head of Treasury



POSITION OVERVIEW

The Director of Trading will own and run Taptap Send's FX trading function across its emerging-market corridors, G10 treasury book, as well as stablecoinon/off ramp. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in emerging-market FX, strong relationships with liquidity providers, sharp analytical and execution skills, and the judgement to manage risk and margin under volatile conditions. This is a live desk doing real volume; the role is to take ownership of it, sharpen what it does, and scale it as corridors grow. The Director of Trading reports to the Head of Treasury and leads the existing trading team.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Trading Execution: Own day-to-day FX execution across 40+ actively traded currencies, setting the standard for execution quality, pricing and risk discipline.

 - P&L Ownership: Take full accountability for trading margin, managing the spread between sourcing cost and required rates corridor by corridor, and reporting performance with clear attribution.

 - Liquidity Management: Build and manage the liquidity provider panel — onboarding, benchmarking and renegotiating to secure competitive pricing — and manage concentration so no corridor depends on a single counterparty.

 - Sourcing Strategy: Optimise how the desk funds each corridor, including stablecoin sourcing where it is cheaper and faster than fiat rails.

 - Treasury Partnership: Work closely with Treasury on pre-funding and working capital, recognising that execution timing moves the funding requirement.

 - Cross-functional Communication: Partner with Ops and Compliance on counterparty risk and settlement flows, and translate FX dynamics into clear decisions for senior stakeholders.

 - Team Leadership: Lead and develop the existing trading team, raising capability and driving automation so the desk scales without scaling headcount one-for-one.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience: A proven track record running FX execution in emerging markets, with direct ownership of a margin or P&L target under volatile conditions. Experience managing liquidity providers and leading a specialist trading team.

 - Skills: Strong command of hedging, exposure limits and the unit economics of cross-border payments. Comfortable with automation, data and modelling. A working view of stablecoins (USDC/USDT) as a sourcing and settlement layer.

Role

As Global Expansion Lead, you will report to the Director of Regulatory Affairs and own and drive our regulatory licensing strategy as we expand into new markets worldwide. You will sit at the intersection of regulation, product, and expansion, working cross-functionally to apply for and obtain regulatory approvals.

You’ll lead licensing applications across multiple jurisdictions, manage relationships with regulators and external advisors, and act as a key internal partner to teams across Legal, Compliance, Product, Operations, and Commercial. This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives in complex regulatory environments and enjoys turning expansion strategy into reality.

Responsibilities

 - Own the end-to-end market entry process for new jurisdictions and products, navigating the approvals and frameworks needed to operate compliantly and at scale.

 - Develop and maintain a global expansion roadmap aligned with the company's growth priorities.

 - Assess feasibility, timelines, costs, and risks for new markets and products — and translate findings into clear recommendations for leadership.

 - Track and interpret market and regulatory developments that impact our international growth and communicate them in practical terms to internal teams.

 - Build scalable frameworks that support new market entry and help us transition smoothly from approval to live operations.

Requirements

 - Direct experience leading license applications in multiple jurisdictions (e.g., UK, EU, Africa, Americas, LATAM, APAC)

 - Strong understanding of cross-border payments, cryptoassets, remittances, and regulated financial products

 - Experience working with regulators and managing regulatory correspondence independently

ABOUT US



We’re building the world’s leading cross-border fintech for emerging markets - empowering families, fueling local economies, and changing how millions access financial services.



Since our launch in 2019, we have transformed how money moves across borders with our operations spanning 6 out of 7 continents and covering both the busiest and most underserved (remittance) corridors. We tapped into a massive shift - from banks to phones - bringing digital finance to places where traditional systems never reached. The next stage of growth will fuel a network of services and products especially designed to make money more accessible to diaspora communities including local cards, and additional savings and payments features.



We’re growing fast, backed by world-class investors, and scaling a product that blends real world impact with cutting edge fintech innovation.



Here’s a little more:

 - Founded by Harvard grads and 3-time founders who previously built Segovia (acquired) and GiveDirectly (>$300M revenue, fastest-growing NGO in the world)

 - Backed by top investors including Spark Capital, Canaan, Reid Hoffman, Breyer Capital, Unbound, Wamda, and more

 - Built by global top performers from Bain, Google, McKinsey, Uber, Checkout, Bolt, and beyond - we move fast, stay humble, and care deeply about our mission

 - Truly global team - 250+ people, 50+ nationalities, 50+ languages

 - Guided by our values - ownership, impact, humility, and heart - in deed, not just word



The Role

Your role will be to boost the growth of TTS in the US-Africa corridor. You will work to continue launching TTS within various African diaspora communities in the United States and build a comprehensive growth (customer acquisition and retention) strategy across the corridor. You will conduct customer research; execute partnerships and events; set up offline and online advertising/brand awareness campaigns; source and coordinate community ambassadors, champions and influencers; measure operational efficiency and financials for the corridor; manage CRM tools; resolve low-level customer complaints, provide CS and Product & Ops teams input into strategies to improve product quality, as well as customer experience. Reporting to the Head of Africa, you'll combine great sales and marketing prowess with a data-driven test-and-learn mindset. You'll be a creative thinker who is always looking for ways to hack growth.

The role offers the chance to join a high growth startup and to have a positive impact on the communities we serve. You will be expected to have deep knowledge of, and networks into African diaspora communities, a passion to support your community, an entrepreneurial spirit, great interpersonal skills, and superb project management.

Responsibilities

 - Build trust-based relationships with African diaspora communities through in-person and online communications

 - Conduct customer research with transfer senders on a regular basis across priority corridors

 - Initiate, negotiate and build partnerships with community associations and organizations representing African diaspora groups

 - Set up offline and online advertising campaigns tailored to specific country communities

 - Source and lead community ambassadors, champions and influencers across African diaspora networks

 - Manage agencies for Paid, Creatives, and Events

 - Manage customers' CRM, segmented by corridor and community

 - Optimize Operations with help from Product, Ops, Eng and Support teams

 - Deep dive into customer pain points by corridor and work with the different teams as well as the Africa Corridor Lead to solve them

 - Travel across US states to connect with and better understand African diaspora communities and grow our customer base

 - Dedicate part of your time to call customers and respond to messages/comments across our owned media channels

 - Work with the Head of Africa to outline the growth plan for each market, including the growth strategy,
